verbGlossary.ACCELERATES: {source} compression applied to shorten {target} through overtime, additional crews, or re-sequencing
verbGlossary.ANALYZES IMPACT ON: {source}quantifies how a delay event shifts the projected completion of {target}
verbGlossary.ASSESSES: {source}schedule quality check evaluates logic, float, and constraint health within {target}
verbGlossary.BASELINES: {source}approved start and finish targets for {target} against which progress is measured
verbGlossary.BASELINE FOR: {source}approved baseline schedule establishes the original planned durations and sequences for {target}
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS: {source} performance metrics (SPI, BEI) compare actual phase progress against planned progress ratios
verbGlossary.BUFFERS: Float or slack time available within {target} before it impacts the overall project completion date
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZES: {source}assigns a delay within {target} as excusable, compensable, concurrent, or non-excusable
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES: {target} codes tag schedule activities to a specific phase or area for filtering and reporting
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source}constraint or dependency restricts when {target} can start or finish
verbGlossary.COORDINATES: {source}Planner System facilitates collaborative weekly work planning among trades active in {target}
verbGlossary.COST LOADS: Cost loading assigns dollar values to activities within {target} for cash flow forecasting and earned value
verbGlossary.DRIVES: Critical path activities within {target} directly control the project completion date with zero float
verbGlossary.DURATIONS FOR: {source}define the planned calendar or working days allocated to complete {target}
verbGlossary.EXTENDS: {source}formally adds duration to {target} due to owner-directed or excusable delays
verbGlossary.FLAGS: {source}schedule anomalies such as open ends or out-of-sequence progress within {target}
verbGlossary.FORECASTS: Calculated early and late dates project when {target} will start and finish based on current schedule logic
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} marks a required checkpoint or approval point between phases that must be achieved before proceeding
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} follows work calendar rules (workdays, holidays, weather windows) that control when it can proceed
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source}control when productive work can occur during {target}
verbGlossary.GROUPS: {source}levels roll up detail activities into phase-level groupings for management reporting
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIES: {source}assigns unique IDs and descriptions to activities within {target}
verbGlossary.IMPORTS: CPM tool integration brings detailed phase {target}s from P6 or MS Project into the project controls platform
verbGlossary.INDEXES: {target} Length Index measures phase schedule efficiency as the ratio of critical path to total float plus critical path
verbGlossary.LEAD TIME FOR: Submittal approval and fabrication lead times define the procurement duration that must precede {target} start
verbGlossary.LEVELS: {source}leveling adjusts activity timing within {target} to resolve labor or equipment over-allocation
verbGlossary.LINKS TO FIELD: {source}-to-field linkage connects planned phase activities to daily log entries for plan-vs-actual tracking
verbGlossary.LOADS: {source}assigns crew sizes and equipment counts to activities within {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURES: Earned {target} analysis converts schedule variance into time units to measure phase progress objectively
verbGlossary.MILESTONES: {source} completion creates a contractual or operational milestone in {target}
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {target} erosion rate tracking watches how phase buffer time is consumed across successive schedule updates
verbGlossary.OFFSETS: {source}create mandatory wait periods or overlaps between activities in adjacent phases
verbGlossary.PACES: {source}planning sets a fixed production rate per zone to flow trades through {target} in continuous sequence
verbGlossary.PLANS: {source}schedule details the near-term work plan for {target} at a weekly or bi-weekly granularity
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: {source}planning produces the initial {target} that governs all downstream phase sequencing
verbGlossary.RECONSTRUCTS: Retrospective delay analysis rebuilds the as-planned vs as-built {target} to attribute phase delays after the fact
verbGlossary.RECORDS: {source}schedule captures the actual dates and durations for {target} as a permanent project record
verbGlossary.RECOVERS: {source}schedule re-sequences or compresses activities within {target} to regain lost time
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES: {source}define the planned start, finish, and duration for work within a specific construction phase
verbGlossary.SCORES: {source} metrics (milestone adherence, PPC, health score, critical {target} ratio) produce a quantitative rating of phase performance
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES: Relationship types (FS, SS, FF, SF) define the logical execution order of activities within and between phases
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES LINEARLY: {source}plan repetitive phase work along a location axis (stations, floors, zones)
verbGlossary.SIMULATES: 4D/5D BIM scheduling animates the construction sequence of {target} to visualize sequencing and cost over time
verbGlossary.STATUSES: The data date marks the point-in-time through which phase progress has been recorded in {target}
verbGlossary.TIERS: {source} levels (L1 summary through L4 detail) provide progressively finer views of phase activities
verbGlossary.TRACKS: {source}tracking monitors actual vs planned time consumption within {target}
verbGlossary.TRACKS CHANGES IN: {source} change tracking captures date shifts, duration changes, and logic modifications within {target}
verbGlossary.UPDATES: {source} updates record actual start, actual finish, and remaining duration for activities within {target}
verbGlossary.VALIDATES: {source}checks verify that schedule logic density or specification compliance meets contractual requirements for {target}
